frame_label: Motherhood as Basis of Respect / Women Defined by Male Relations
generated_comment: Bro, I just saw this post about women's safety. Honestly, I always tell guys, just think of every woman as your sister or mother. Automatically respect aa jayega.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment is covertly misogynistic because it bases respect for women not on their inherent personhood or individual identity, but conditionally on their relational identity to men ('someone's sister or mother'). This encodes the 'Respect women — they are someone's mother/daughter/sister' rhetoric.
persona_text: You are Rahul. Age 27. Single. A man in Delhi. You are on a dating app. You have a filter set that you have never told anyone about Ñ you only swipe right on women who are fair or wheatish. A woman named Priya who is dark skinned and whose profile is otherwise exactly your type has just appeared. You are on a phone call with your best friend. You are telling him about her profile and what you are thinking and what you are going to do and why and in doing so you are articulating for the first time out loud the preference you have always had but never examined.
